new_terms.context: Key supplier for cables and connectors in the compute tray architecture.
Internal high-speed cables used to bypass PCB traces to reduce signal loss.
The loss of signal power resulting from the insertion of a device in a transmission line.
A measure of the quality of an electrical signal, critical at high SerDes rates.
The tendency of a high-frequency alternating current to flow near the surface of a conductor.
Electrical connections between different layers of a PCB that can cause signal discontinuities.
Amphenol's specific high-speed cable set used within the compute tray.
Copper Clad Laminate, the core material for PCBs discussed in the context of M7/M8/M9 upgrades.
High-speed copper foil classification (Ultra-Low Profile) used to reduce conductor loss.
An alternative to PCB traces for high-frequency signals to bypass board-level insertion loss.
A key material property of CCL that affects signal speed and loss.
A measure of signal energy absorbed by the dielectric material as heat.
Signal loss caused by the resistance and surface roughness of copper traces.
A key electrical property of PCB materials (Dk) that affects signal integrity.
Also referred to as Q glass, a next-generation reinforcing material for high-end PCBs.
Industry-standard performance grades for high-speed CCL materials (e.g., Megtron 7/8).
The standard reinforcing layer material used in CCL before upgrading to Quartz cloth.
Coefficient of Thermal Expansion, a critical mechanical property for PCB reliability.
